prompt:
Partner API started enforcing quotas for real this week:

  429 Too Many Requests — quota: 30 requests per rolling 10s — retry-after: 4

'Rolling' is the operative word — our fixed-bucket limiter bursts 60 across a bucket boundary and they still 429 us. Build ratelimit.ts exporting class SlidingWindowLimiter.

Constructor takes { limit, windowMs, timers? }. Semantics: an acquisition holds a slot for windowMs from the moment it was GRANTED (not requested), and at most `limit` grants may exist inside any rolling window. acquire(signal?) resolves immediately while slots are free, otherwise queues; queued callers get slots strictly FIFO as old grants age out (a grant from t is free again at exactly t+windowMs; several can free at once). AbortSignal support because our HTTP layer times out: pre-aborted signal rejects with its reason without queueing; aborting while queued rejects the waiter, drops it from the queue, and must NOT burn a slot or disturb the order of everyone behind it. Also tryAcquire(): boolean — grab a free slot synchronously or return false, never queue, never jump ahead of queued waiters. Expose `pending` (number of queued waiters). Throw TypeError from the constructor for a non-positive-integer limit or windowMs <= 0.

timers is { now, setTimeout, clearTimeout } defaulting to the real ones; the acceptance tests (test_ratelimit.ts, `node --test test_ratelimit.ts`) drive a fake clock through it, so all waiting must go through timers — no Date.now() or bare setTimeout anywhere. Tests are the contract; don't edit them. No dependencies.
